figures.caption: Figure 1: MASS pipeline. The generator parameterized by θ generates m auxiliary training examples (p1, a1), . . . , (pm, am) given the target task T . The examples are assigned scores using the scorer parameterized by η. Then, θ performs a weighted SFT self-update on the synthetic data to produce an adapted model θ′ before producing resposne R̂ in attempting the target problem. Metagradients computed by backpropagating target performance through the inner updates are used to update η to identify examples that help the downstream loss and reward θ to generate better examples.
Table 1: Accuracy of methods on MATH-500.
